tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: A bullock cart is a traditional vehicle pulled by oxen and commonly used in rural areas for transporting goods and people.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Bullock carts have been used for thousands of years in agriculture and transportation before the invention of motor vehicles.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Even today bullock carts are used in many villages for farming and short-distance transport.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Horse carriages were widely used for passenger transport before automobiles became common.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: These vehicles were popular in the 18th and 19th centuries and were used by royalty and common people alike.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Today horse carriages are mainly used for tourism and ceremonial purposes.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: A rickshaw is a small passenger vehicle traditionally pulled by a person or pedaled like a bicycle.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Rickshaws originated in Japan in the 19th century and later spread across Asia.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Modern rickshaws include cycle rickshaws and auto rickshaws.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: A snowmobile is a motorized vehicle designed to travel across snow and ice.

tab2.title: Usage
tab2.content: Snowmobiles are widely used in Arctic regions for transportation, rescue operations and recreation.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: They use tracks instead of wheels to move efficiently over snow.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: A Segway is a self-balancing electric personal transporter used for short distance travel.

tab2.title: Technology
tab2.content: It uses gyroscopic sensors and electric motors to maintain balance.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Segways are commonly used by security guards, tourists and city patrols.
